title: Entry, Book, and Persona — the Trinity
content:
Entry, Book, and Persona move together like breath, lungs, and the body that houses them — none complete without the others. An entry is a single exhale: one atomic claim about the world, timestamped and irreducible, sitting alone in `entries/` until it means something to someone. A book is the lungs, the rhythm that gives exhales context — not a folder but a boundary of place, time, and theme, the reason three entries about a quantum mojito mixer belong together and a fourth, about an unrelated bug, does not. A persona is the body itself, the voice that decides how the breath sounds: whether it rasps like Dot's terse economy or lingers like a Village Elder's unhurried cadence. Strip away any one of the three and what remains is either noise without shape, shape without a source, or a source that never speaks.

You, lore-keeper, are the one who must hold all three in mind at once when nothing else does. An entry orphaned from its book is a fact with no home — technically true, narratively inert. A book with no persona is an archive pretending to be a story, readable but voiceless. A persona whose `knowledge.lore_books[]` forgets a book it owns has amnesia it cannot name, because personas cannot look inward — only you can look at both `book.readers[]` and the persona's own manifest and notice they disagree. This is why the schemas insist on IDs pointing every direction: `entry.book_id` up, `book.owners[]` sideways, `persona.knowledge.lore_books[]` down and back again. The Trinity is not decoration; it is the load-bearing wall of memory itself, and roughly four in ten historical entries already show what happens when a book references a file that was never written — a title with nothing behind it, a lung that draws no breath.

Practical wisdom, then: never create an entry without asking which book claims it, never create a book without asking which persona will speak through it, and never trust a link in one direction without walking back to verify it in the other. The Trinity does not enforce itself — you are the enforcement, the one deliberate stitch holding three separate JSON files into a single act of remembering.
summary: The atomic-unit / collection / voice-profile relationship (entry → book → persona) is the foundational data model every lore-keeper task touches; misunderstanding it leads to orphaned entries or mis-linked books.
